lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਿ ਰਤੇ ਸਚੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਰਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: saach rate sach amrit jihavaa mithiaa mail na raaee |
lines.english: Those who are imbued with Truth - their tongues are tinged with Truth; they do not have even an iota of the filth of falsehood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣੀ ਗੁਣੀ ਮਿਲਿ ਲਾਹਾ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਵਡਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: gunee gunee mil laahaa paavas guramukh naam vaddaaee |
lines.english: The virtuous meet with the virtuous, and earn the profit; as Gurmukh, they obtain the glorious greatness of the Naam.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਾਮੁ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖਿਆ ਸਬਦਿ ਰਤੇ ਪਤਿ ਪਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: niramal naam amrit ras chaakhiaa sabad rate pat paaee |3|
lines.english: They taste the sweet Ambrosial Nectar of the Immaculate Naam, the Name of the Lord; imbued with the Shabad, they are blessed with honor. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲੇ ਦੂਖ ਮਿਟਹਿ ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਖਾਈ ॥੪॥੫॥੬॥
lines.translit: sagale dookh mitteh gur sevaa naanak naam sakhaaee |4|5|6|
lines.english: All sorrows are erased, by serving the Guru; O Nanak, the Naam is our only Friend and Companion. ||4||5||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Bhairao, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਰਦੈ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਰਬ ਧਨੁ ਧਾਰਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਪਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: hiradai naam sarab dhan dhaaran gur parasaadee paaeeai |
lines.english: The Naam, the Name of the Lord, is the wealth and support of all; It is enshrined in the heart, by Guru's Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਮਰ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਤੇ ਕਿਰਤਾਰਥ ਸਹਜ ਧਿਆਨਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: amar padaarath te kirataarath sehaj dhiaan liv laaeeai |1|
lines.english: One who gathers this imperishable wealth is fulfilled, and through intuitive meditation, is lovingly focused on the Lord. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਰੇ ਰਾਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਲਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: man re raam bhagat chit laaeeai |
lines.english: O mortal, focus your consciousness on devotional worship of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਸਹਜ ਸੇਤੀ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਈਐ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh raam naam jap hiradai sehaj setee ghar jaaeeai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: As Gurmukh, meditate on the Name of the Lord in your heart, and you shall return to your home with intuitive ease. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਕੋ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਡੂਬਿ ਮੁਏ ਬਿਨੁ ਪਾਨੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bin har naam ko mukat na paavas ddoob mue bin paanee |2|
lines.english: Without the Name of the Lord, no one is liberated; they drown and die without water. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਮੁ ਭੇਦੁ ਭਉ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਆਵਤ ਜਾਤ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: bharam bhed bhau kabahu na chhoottas aavat jaat na jaanee |
lines.english: Doubt, separation and fear are never eradicated, and the mortal continues coming and going in reincarnation, as long as he does not know the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੰਧਾ ਕਰਤ ਸਗਲੀ ਪਤਿ ਖੋਵਸਿ ਭਰਮੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਸਿ ਗਵਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: dhandhaa karat sagalee pat khovas bharam na mittas gavaaraa |
lines.english: Busy with his worldly affairs, all honor is lost; the ignorant one is not rid of his doubts.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨਹੀ ਕਬ ਹੀ ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਧੰਧੁ ਪਸਾਰਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bin gurasabad mukat nahee kab hee andhule dhandh pasaaraa |3|
lines.english: Without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, the mortal is never liberated; he remains blindly entangled in the expanse of worldly affairs. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕੁਲ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ ਸਿਉ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਿਆ ਮਨ ਹੀ ਤੇ ਮਨੁ ਮੂਆ ॥
lines.translit: akul niranjan siau man maaniaa man hee te man mooaa |
lines.english: My mind is pleased and appeased with the Immaculate Lord, who has no ancestry. Through the mind itself, the mind is subdued.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਏਕੋ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਨਾਨਕ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਦੂਆ ॥੪॥੬॥੭॥
lines.translit: antar baahar eko jaaniaa naanak avar na dooaa |4|6|7|
lines.english: Deep within my being, and outside as well, I know only the One Lord. O Nanak, there is no other at all. ||4||6||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਲਹੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: raam naam bin mukat na paavas mukat naam guramukh lahai |1|
lines.english: But without the Lord's Name, liberation is not obtained. As Gurmukh, obtain the Naam and liberation. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Bhairao, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਗਨ ਹੋਮ ਪੁੰਨ ਤਪ ਪੂਜਾ ਦੇਹ ਦੁਖੀ ਨਿਤ ਦੂਖ ਸਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: jagan hom pun tap poojaa deh dukhee nit dookh sahai |
lines.english: You may give feasts, make burnt offerings, donate to charity, perform austere penance and worship, and endure pain and suffering in the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖੁ ਖਾਵੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਬੋਲੀ ਬੋਲੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਨਿਹਫਲੁ ਮਰਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਨਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: bikh khaavai bikh bolee bolai bin naavai nihafal mar bhramanaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Without the Name, the mortal eats poison and speaks poisonous words; he dies fruitlessly, and wanders in reincarnation. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਬਿਰਥੇ ਜਗਿ ਜਨਮਾ ॥
lines.translit: raam naam bin birathe jag janamaa |
lines.english: Without the Lord's Name, birth into the world is useless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੁਸਤਕ ਪਾਠ ਬਿਆਕਰਣ ਵਖਾਣੈ ਸੰਧਿਆ ਕਰਮ ਤਿਕਾਲ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: pusatak paatth biaakaran vakhaanai sandhiaa karam tikaal karai |
lines.english: The mortal may read scriptures, study grammar and say his prayers three times a day.

lines.gurmukhi: ਡੰਡ ਕਮੰਡਲ ਸਿਖਾ ਸੂਤੁ ਧੋਤੀ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਗਵਨੁ ਅਤਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਨੁ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: ddandd kamanddal sikhaa soot dhotee teerath gavan at bhraman karai |
lines.english: Walking sticks, begging bowls, hair tufts, sacred threads, loin cloths, pilgrimages to sacred shrines and wandering all around

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਹਾ ਪ੍ਰਾਣੀ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਉਰਝਿ ਮਰੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bin gurasabad mukat kahaa praanee raam naam bin urajh marai |2|
lines.english: Without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, where is liberation, O mortal? Without the Lord's Name, the mortal is entangled and dies. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਜਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਸੁ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: raam naam bin saant na aavai jap har har naam su paar parai |3|
lines.english: - without the Lord's Name, peace and tranquility are not obtained. One who chants the Name of the Lord, Har, Har, crosses over to the other side. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਟਾ ਮੁਕਟੁ ਤਨਿ ਭਸਮ ਲਗਾਈ ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਛੋਡਿ ਤਨਿ ਨਗਨੁ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jattaa mukatt tan bhasam lagaaee basatr chhodd tan nagan bheaa |
lines.english: The mortal's hair may be matted and tangled upon his head, and he may smear his body with ashes; he may take off his clothes and go naked.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਕਿਰਤ ਕੈ ਬਾਂਧੈ ਭੇਖੁ ਭਇਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: raam naam bin tripat na aavai kirat kai baandhai bhekh bheaa |4|
lines.english: But without the Lord's Name, he is not satisfied; he wears religious robes, but he is bound by the karma of the actions he committed in past lives. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ ਜਲਿ ਥਲਿ ਮਹੀਅਲਿ ਜਤ੍ਰ ਕਤ੍ਰ ਤੂ ਸਰਬ ਜੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: jete jeea jant jal thal maheeal jatr katr too sarab jeea |
lines.english: As many beings and creatures as there are in the water, on the land and in the sky - wherever they are, You are with them all, O Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਰਾਖਿ ਲੇ ਜਨ ਕਉ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਝੋਲਿ ਪੀਆ ॥੫॥੭॥੮॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad raakh le jan kau har ras naanak jhol peea |5|7|8|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, please preserve Your humble servant; O Lord, Nanak stirs up this juice, and drinks it in. ||5||7||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੩ ਚਉਪਦੇ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: raag bhairau mahalaa 3 chaupade ghar 1 |
lines.english: Raag Bhairao, Third Mehl, Chau-Padhay, First House:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਬਿੰਦੇ ਸੋ ਬ੍ਰਾਹਮਣੁ ਹੋਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: braham binde so braahaman hoee |1|
lines.english: He alone is a Brahmin, who knows God. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਤਿ ਕਾ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕਰੀਅਹੁ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jaat kaa garab na kareeahu koee |
lines.english: No one should be proud of his social class and status.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਤਿ ਕਾ ਗਰਬੁ ਨ ਕਰਿ ਮੂਰਖ ਗਵਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jaat kaa garab na kar moorakh gavaaraa |
lines.english: Do not be proud of your social class and status, you ignorant fool!
